selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

We've all seen those viral videos or posts that just spread misinformation and chaos. And it's not just the content itself, it's the way it affects people's mental health. I've seen friends get obsessed with their online presence, constantly comparing themselves to others and feeling like they're not good enough. It's like, we're losing touch with reality here. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a delivery driver in Bologna, I've developed a thick skin over the years, but even I get frustrated when scrolling through social media and coming across fake content. It's like, what's the point of even having a platform if we can't trust what we're seeing? I've talked to friends and family about this, and we all agree that it's getting out of hand. We've all seen those viral videos or posts that just spread misinformation and chaos. And it's not just the content itself, it's the way it affects people's mental health. I've seen friends get obsessed with their online presence, constantly comparing themselves to others and feeling like they're not good enough. It's like, we're losing touch with reality here. So, how do we deal with this? First of all, we need to be more aware of what we're consuming online. Take a step back and think, 'Is this really true?' or 'Is this just someone trying to get attention?' We need to be more discerning and critical thinkers. And if we see something that's clearly false, we need to call out the offender. No more spreading hate or misinformation without consequences. It's time for us to take control of our online spaces and make them more positive and respectful. We can start by having more open and honest conversations with each other, sharing our own experiences and perspectives, and creating content that's authentic and meaningful. It's not going to be easy, but we owe it to ourselves and each other to make a change.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I've observed the issue of fake content on social media firsthand, having witnessed it affect my friends and family. I've also noticed its impact on my own mental health, feeling anxious and uncertain when scrolling through my feeds. I've decided that I need to take a more active role in addressing this issue, starting with being more mindful of the content I consume and engaging in conversations with others about the importance of authenticity and respect online. I've come to realize that we all have a responsibility to create positive change, and that starts with our individual actions and choices.
